subject: Biology
title: How Plants Eat: Sugar Transport
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on How Plants Eat: Sugar Transport.

hook.text: Plants make sugar in their leaves. But roots, fruits, and flowers need sugar too.

buildup.text: Phloem tubes transport sugars from leaves to every other part of the plant.

discovery.text: Unlike xylem, phloem moves materials in both directions—up and down—depending on need.

twist.text: Aphids tap into phloem like tiny vampires, drinking sugar sap directly from plant veins.

climax.text: Scientists discovered phloem transport by studying the drops of sap that leaked from severed aphid mouthparts.

punchline.text: Plants feed themselves through a hidden highway of sugar.

quiz.question: What do phloem tubes transport?
options: Sugars from leaves to other parts
